beats.activity: Wake up, coffee on the patio with the water view, checking the weather.
beats.reasoning: Saturday morning ritual—patio coffee is Andre's anchor. Low energy yesterday (40/100) means a gentle, grounded start. Overcast weather keeps the patio pleasant without intense heat.
Extending the home calm; no rush on a Saturday. Reading aligns with his bookish nature and contemplative mood.
Cross-town drive from Leschi to Ballard (~6 mi, 25 min). Farmers markets are in full swing today; Andre enjoys local, fresh ingredients for cooking and the slower pace of Saturday markets.

beats.activity: Browse farmers market, pick up fresh vegetables and flowers.
beats.reasoning: Overcast weather is perfect for outdoor market browsing without heat stress. Andre values local, seasonal ingredients and the sensory experience of markets—fits his generous, present-oriented nature.

beats.activity: Drive to Capitol Hill, stop at Elliott Bay Books.
beats.location_name: Elliott Bay Book Company (Capitol Hill, Seattle)
beats.reasoning: Ballard to Capitol Hill is ~15 min drive; Elliott Bay is his sanctuary. He's been there multiple days in a row recently—the pattern shows it's essential to his week. Low energy suggests he needs the grounding of browsing and the staff's recommendations.

beats.activity: Wander the stacks, chat with staff, find a new poetry collection or surprise book.
beats.location_name: Elliott Bay Book Company (Capitol Hill, Seattle)
beats.reasoning: Elliott Bay is Andre's contemplative refuge; he spends real time there. Overcast weather means no rush to leave. This beat recharges him after yesterday's low energy.

beats.activity: Lunch at Serious Pie, grab a slice and coffee.
beats.reasoning: Serious Pie is his Capitol Hill go-to (visited multiple recent days). Walking distance from Elliott Bay. Casual Saturday lunch fits his rhythm and doesn't require planning.

Capitol Hill to Leschi is ~15 min drive. Andre values close friends and quiet time over big crowds; the Mariners game (1pm at T-Mobile) doesn't fit his profile—he'd skip the SoDo crowds. Heading home aligns with his preference for contemplative afternoons.

beats.activity: Prepare lunch and work in the pottery studio, hands in clay.
beats.reasoning: Saturday afternoon pottery is Andre's creative anchor—mentioned every recent day. Low energy yesterday suggests he needs extended unstructured time. No pressure, just shaping; this is his recharge.

beats.activity: Shower, prepare dinner with fresh market vegetables, eat on the patio.
beats.reasoning: Fresh ingredients from Ballard market get used tonight. Patio dinner is his Saturday ritual—quiet, grounded, water view. Overcast weather still allows comfortable outdoor time.

beats.activity: Read new poetry collection on the patio or indoors, journal a bit.
beats.reasoning: Elliott Bay book purchase gets read. Poetry and journaling fit his reflective nature. Evening settling in at home, no evening plans—he values close friends, not random Saturday socializing.

beats.activity: Light tidying, prepare for bed, read in bed.
beats.reasoning: Wind-down beat—already at home, no commute before sleep. Quiet evening routine, early bedtime after a full day of movement.
7.5 hours sleep, ending at 6:30 AM Sunday morning. Beat spans overnight as required. Regenerative rest after yesterday's low energy.

world_event_prompt: Overcast Saturday, high of 64F. Farmers markets in Ballard, Fremont, and the U-District in full swing. Mariners home game at T-Mobile Park at 1pm brings SoDo crowds.
